title: pull image looping until error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When pulling some images, it will run in loop. When pulling is done, it will pull again, and again, until there's an error. The error is different each time. Also, error are not the same depending on the storage driver, but the loop is always there...

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I guess it depends on the image, and I can't share the image unfortunately... It's the first image I have the issue with.
However, there are a lot of (too much) layers, but steps are standard (mainly RUN and ADD)

**Describe the results you received:**

Pull loop (see full log below)

**Describe the results you expected:**

No pull loop

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens only on this image (for now)
Image is on a private registry using artifactory
docker pull is working fine.
the full image is 1.5 GB.
the error may vary at each run, also, changing the storage driver also changes the error, but the error still happens.
The logs below happens with vfs storage.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @rhatdan @QiWang19 Is this the pull retry code?
Retry should stop if pull succeeded. Is it possible it retries because of the error `no such file or directory`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I have seen this problem with the user namespace is not setup to handle some UIDs.  Could this be the issue?

For me the issue is the lack of free volume on disk.
But I would say no matter the reason, there may be many, but it should be gracefuly handled... Retrying is fine, but for a limited number of retries. And the error should be explicite. If it's the lack of free space, then say it. If it's something else, then say it.
It could be a long running improvement, as unknown cases are discovered... But the first step would be to retry a few times, and stop with an error message. If the reason is given, it's even better :)

Anyway, it's not blocker for me anymore, I stopped using podman because of this issue. I will give more insight.

I was using podman to pull images inside a docker image, to scan it with trivy, in the context of GitLab-CI with an unprivileged gitlab-runner docker executor, so docker is not an option. However, in this case, only the VFS driver is possible, which download each layer as a full size folder, explaining the lack of disk space. Let's say you have a 10 layers 1 GB image, it will take up to 10 GB  on your disk. I was not able to find a workaround with podman, but I have found a better fix : scanning the image from the image itself. So no need anymore to pull the image to scan, the job is executed in this very image...

I see the same when pulling the official nextcloud image. The official httpd and the official Hello World image works (both images, pull and run successfully)

The issue is caused by not enough disk space. (I provided 10GB of free space)
Nextcloud is bigger than the other two, but does it need 10GB for pulling the NC image? According to portainer using docker the NC image is about 800 MB. 

Can it be that through copying repeatedly the drive is filled? (That is why I post my issue here - and because I also see repeated pulling output in the console just like above)

Can someone try to pull the official NC image?

No root used, user namespace used.
Debian 10 buster 10.6.
Configs of podman left default (VFA used according to storage folder)
Podman version 2.1.1

@QiWang19 @vrothberg @mtrmac Any idea what is going on here.  Seems like we have a loop on failure.  Is this retry or something else?  Is it in containers/image or in podman?

@topas-rec @karfau Could you try podman pull to see if the same failures happen, Even better could you try with `buildah pull`

>Could you try podman pull to see if the same failures

That is what caused my issue. 

I will also try `buildah pull`

> @karfau Could you try podman pull to see if the same failures happen, Even better could you try with `buildah pull`

Not sure why you are asking me, did you mean to mention somebody else?

If you think it would be helpful for you to know what happens when I execute `buildah pull nextcloud` let me know and I can install it and try.

I freed again some space (about 12 GB were free) and restarted the pull and **it succeeded**.
So the repeating was just caused by no space on device which was mentioned in the error. Here is a log of the failed pull (no space left):

Image size seems to be about 800 MB but my hard drive is at 4 GB free space. Podman storage grew to 8.4 GB:
![Screenshot from 2020-11-18 17-37-55](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20391449/99559276-e3604c80-29c4-11eb-87cf-aa5c0337a31e.png)

Should I create an issue for the used storage size or is this usual (and why?)?

Thanks, we have also seen similar issues with a UID not being defined within the user namespace of the user doing the pull.  Basically the `chown` to a UID outside of the namespace fails, and we look and start pulling the image again.

> However, in this case, only the VFS driver is possible, which download each layer as a full size folder, explaining the lack of disk space. Let's say you have a 10 layers 1 GB image, it will take up to 10 GB on your disk.

Yes, VFS amplifies the disk space requirements significantly.

A few issues to look into here:
- If the storage (driver?) fails because it is out of space, it would be nice to report that as out of space and not ENOENT.
- From a quick skim, the retry code seems to retry on any `syscall.Errno` that is not ECONNREFUSED; that should probably be more specific (or maybe even default to not retrying and only retry on specific known errors (and wouldn’t ECONNREFUSED be one of those to retry? Tracing back the rationale might be non-trivial work)).
- The retry code has a `logrus.Infof("Warning: failed…")`, but that does not show up in any of the logs above. Why / how to change that?
- That `Warning: failed` message should probably include the error cause from that attempt; we currently discard all but the last cause.
- Extra credit (API changes through the stack, and a fair amount of work): Estimate disk space requirements for storing an image and abort before making any layer copies if it is obvious it won’t fit.
I opened the above PR to implement some of @mtrmac suggested improvements.
